title: DLRS Entity Graph Node
description: A single node in the v0.6 personal knowledge graph (a person, organisation, location, event, etc.). Nodes are produced by pipelines/knowledge_graph/ from cleaned text and / or memory atoms. Like memory atoms, every node MUST be redaction-safe at write time: any value that would have triggered the v0.5 redaction pass (email, phone, ID, etc.) MUST be substituted with its category placeholder before being stored as a label or alias. v0.6 introduces this schema; the emitter lands in issue #57.

schema_version.description: Version of this schema. Bump on any breaking change.

node_id.description: Stable identifier for this node within the owning record. ULID or UUIDv4. MUST be immutable once written; re-running the pipeline produces a new node_id even when the entity is the same, so historical edges and audit trails remain valid.

record_id.description: Owning DLRS record identifier. Same record_id pattern as the rest of DLRS so nodes can be re-attributed, redacted, or erased on consent withdrawal.

kind.description: Coarse entity kind. The set is intentionally small so that downstream tooling can mechanically partition the graph; finer-grained typing belongs in the label / aliases or in pipeline-specific parameters on the descriptor, not here.

label.description: Canonical human-readable label for the entity (the form most likely to be used for display and matching). MUST be redaction-safe; if the recognised name itself is sensitive (e.g. it matches a redaction rule), the pipeline MUST substitute the category placeholder before writing.

aliases.description: Other surface forms by which the entity is referred to in the source text. MAY be empty. Each alias MUST be redaction-safe.

salience.description: Importance of this entity in the source corpus, in [0, 1]. Deterministic baseline backends MAY pin this to a heuristic (e.g. mention_count / max_mentions); model-based backends should propagate the model's own salience score.

sensitivity.description: Sensitivity tier inherited from the source record's manifest. Nodes MAY NOT be promoted to a less restrictive tier than their source.

redaction_safe.description: Schema-enforced const true. Pipelines MUST run their label and aliases through the v0.5 redaction pass before writing. Consumers SHOULD reject any node where redaction_safe != true.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the node was emitted.

pipeline_version.description: Version of the pipeline that emitted the node. SemVer, or 'git:<sha>' when the pipeline is built directly from a commit.

audit_event_ref.description: Optional pointer to the audit/events.jsonl line emitted when this node was written. Format: 'audit/events.jsonl#L<n>'. Populated by the descriptor->audit bridge (issue #58).
